body_txt: A lot of characters need (or want) things they don't have — usually vehicles, weapons, or money. Despite obtaining these items through what can only be described as theft, they often don't percieve it as stealing (or are unwilling to admit it). So what do they say when confronted? "I borrowed it." Sometimes they will casually acknowledge that they borrowed it without asking. Sometimes they'll note that the owner is unaware it was borrowed. Sometimes they will claim that they intended to return it, but was unable due to circumstance beyond their control. Except them to play Rules Lawyer over the definition of theft, or borrowing. Children's media also often has the Stock Aesop that "borrowing without asking" is functionally identical to theft . Sub-Trope to Insistent Terminology . Related to Hero Stole My Bike and Fell Off the Back of a Truck . Compare "Blackmail" Is Such an Ugly Word , "Not Illegal" Justification , and Less Embarrassing Term . Also see How Did You Get It? .
